hyperedges.label: PINN v8 Complete Experiment Suite
hyperedges.rationale: All 5 plots in the PINN/10/ directory collectively document the complete v8 model experiment: training dynamics, physics constraints, and evaluation metrics

hyperedges.label: PINN Physics-Informed Constraint Diagnostics
hyperedges.rationale: These plots collectively capture the physics-informed neural network constraint enforcement: gradient verification, lambda scheduling, and loss decomposition

hyperedges.label: Energy Gradient Evolution across v11-v13
hyperedges.observation: v11 peak at -0.014, v12 broader bell at -0.020, v13 sharp spike near 0 — indicating progressive correction of muon energy gradient toward physical neutrality

hyperedges.label: Uncertainty Calibration Evolution across v11-v13
hyperedges.observation: All three versions show approximately Gaussian normalized residuals centered near 0; v13 uses blue histogram (style change); width slightly broadens from v11 to v12

hyperedges.label: PINN Iteration 5 Experiment
hyperedges.note: All four images originate from PINN subfolder 5, representing a single training run. RMSE=0.106, automatic lambda balancing, fully positive gradients.

hyperedges.label: PINN Iteration 6 Experiment
hyperedges.note: All four images originate from PINN subfolder 6. RMSE=0.202, fixed warm-up lambda schedule, collapsed gradient — worst-performing iteration.

hyperedges.label: PINN Iteration 7 Experiment
hyperedges.note: Both images originate from PINN subfolder 7. RMSE=0.110, no uncertainty head, partial monotonicity violations, partial recovery from iter 6 degradation.

hyperedges.label: Gradient Diagnostic Series (Iters 5-7)
hyperedges.note: Three consecutive iterations of dE/d(log10 Nmu) histograms showing progression: healthy (iter 5) → collapsed (iter 6) → partial recovery with violations (iter 7).

hyperedges.label: RMSE Progression Across Iterations 5-7
hyperedges.note: RMSE values: 0.106 (iter 5, +Unc v2) → 0.202 (iter 6, +Unc v4) → 0.110 (iter 7, no Unc). Iter 6 is anomalously poor; iter 7 partially recovers.

nodes.label: GRAPES-3 MC vs Data Distributions
nodes.description: Five-panel comparison of Monte Carlo simulations vs observed data distributions for GRAPES-3 cosmic ray shower parameters: log10(Ne) electron count, log10(Nmu) muon count, Shower Age, cos(theta) zenith angle, and log10(E/eV) energy. MC (blue) and Data (red) histograms plotted on log-density scale showing general agreement with some discrepancies.
nodes.insight: MC and data broadly agree on shower age and zenith angle; notable discrepancy in Ne and Nmu tails suggests imperfect hadronic model tuning

nodes.label: Surface Fit Residuals vs Energy
nodes.description: Two-panel 2D density scatter plot. Left: surface fit residuals (True minus Fitted) vs log10(E) showing a symmetric elliptical distribution centered near zero. Right: discrete banding test for integer Nmu artefact showing vertical stripe patterns indicating quantization artefacts in muon count reconstruction.
nodes.metric: Residual = True - Fitted energy surface; Count density
nodes.insight: Fit residuals are centered and symmetric indicating unbiased surface fit; integer Nmu banding reveals quantization artefact in low-muon events

nodes.label: Gaussian Curvature vs Energy
nodes.description: Three-panel figure. Left: Gaussian curvature K vs log10(E) showing near-zero values. Center: Decay of principal curvatures |k1| and |k2| magnitudes vs log10(E) with a sign reversal flagged around logE=13.2. Right: extensive annotation table of k2 sign reversal events at various energy bins, suggesting a structural feature (knee region) in the energy-dependent curvature surface.
nodes.metric: Gaussian curvature K, principal curvatures k1 and k2
nodes.insight: Sign reversal of k2 near logE~13.2 corresponds to the cosmic ray knee region; curvature analysis reveals non-linear structure in the energy reconstruction surface

nodes.label: Alpha Energy Exponent Map (Shower Age vs cos theta)
nodes.description: 2D heatmap of energy exponent alpha as a function of shower age s (y-axis, 0.8 to 1.8) and cos(theta) (x-axis, 0.75 to 1.0). Color scale from ~0.11 to 0.28. Title notes 'Paper ignores this!' indicating the published analysis omits the dependence of alpha on both shower age and zenith angle, which shows significant variation especially at extreme values.
nodes.insight: Alpha varies significantly with shower age and zenith angle (range 0.11 to 0.28); ignoring this 2D dependence introduces systematic errors in energy estimation

nodes.label: Energy Resolution and Bias
nodes.description: Two-panel plot. Left: Energy resolution sigma(log10 E) as a function of true log10(E), shown as a flat dashed red line at sigma=0.1059 (matching paper value of 0.1059). Right: Energy bias (Reco - True) vs true log10(E) showing zero bias across the energy range. Both plots indicate stable and unbiased energy reconstruction.
nodes.insight: Energy reconstruction is unbiased with constant resolution of ~0.106 dex in log10(E), consistent with the published paper value

nodes.label: 3D Quadratic Scaling Surface
nodes.description: 3D scatter plot and fitted surface showing the quadratic energy scaling relationship. Parameters: beta=0.422, gamma=0.00235. Axes are log(Ne), log(Nmu), and log(E). A planar-like surface (with slight curvature) fits through the data cloud, demonstrating the quadratic correction to the linear Ne-Nmu energy estimator.
nodes.insight: The quadratic term (gamma) is small but non-negligible; the dominant energy dependence comes from the linear combination of log(Ne) and log(Nmu) with coefficient beta~0.42

nodes.label: Zenith-Dependent Energy Resolution and Bias
nodes.description: Two-panel plot showing energy resolution and bias as a function of zenith angle (cos theta from ~0.78 to 0.98). Left: sigma(log E) vs cos(theta) showing essentially zero variation (flat at ~0). Right: Energy bias vs cos(theta) also flat at zero. Both panels demonstrate no zenith-dependent systematic error in energy reconstruction.
nodes.metric: sigma(log E) and Bias vs cos(theta)
nodes.insight: No zenith-dependent systematic bias or resolution degradation detected, confirming atmospheric depth corrections are working correctly

nodes.label: Principal Curvatures vs Energy (Knee Region)
nodes.description: Line plot of principal curvatures kappa1 (dominant, blue) and kappa2 (secondary, orange) vs mean log10(E) in energy bins from 12.4 to 13.6. kappa1 shows large negative values at low energy (~-0.57 at logE=12.4), rises to positive peak (~+0.24 at logE=13.2), then drops to ~-0.36 at logE=13.6. kappa2 remains near zero throughout. Red dashed line marks zero. The sign change of kappa1 near logE=13.2 aligns with the cosmic ray knee.
nodes.metric: Principal curvatures kappa1 and kappa2 vs mean log10(E)
nodes.insight: kappa1 sign reversal near logE~13.2 is a geometric indicator of the spectral knee feature in the energy reconstruction surface

nodes.label: Uncertainty Calibration Scatter Plot
nodes.description: Scatter plot of |Error| vs Predicted sigma for uncertainty calibration. The majority of points are clustered at predicted sigma ~0.23-0.29 with errors ranging from 0 to 0.5, with a dense vertical band near sigma~0.25. A few outliers appear at sigma~0.52 with moderate errors. Demonstrates that the model predicts a narrow range of uncertainty values but actual errors span a wide range.
nodes.insight: The predicted sigma is poorly discriminative (clustered at ~0.25 for most events), suggesting the uncertainty estimator has low dynamic range and may underfit heteroscedastic noise

nodes.label: Reliability Curve for Uncertainty Estimation
nodes.description: Reliability (calibration) curve showing Mean |error| vs Predicted sigma. Blue solid line shows actual model behavior rising from ~0.103 at sigma~0.239 to ~0.140 at sigma~0.259. Orange dashed line shows ideal calibration (perfect calibration diagonal). The actual curve lies well below the ideal line, indicating that the model's predicted uncertainties are overestimated relative to actual errors.
nodes.metric: Mean |error| vs Predicted sigma; ideal vs actual calibration
nodes.insight: The uncertainty model overestimates errors (actual mean error below ideal line), meaning the predicted sigmas are conservative/inflated relative to true reconstruction accuracy

nodes.trend: Negative curvature increasing toward zero as log E increases from 12.4 to 13.2, then slight decrease

nodes.label: Multiplicity Growth vs Primary Mass
nodes.trend: Nearly flat delta ~0.078 across all primary masses, indicating weak mass dependence

nodes.label: Surface Fit Residuals
nodes.trend: Residuals spread from 0 to -1.2 with fan-like structure at higher energies; asymmetric distribution indicating systematic underestimation at high energy

nodes.label: Neural Network Training History
nodes.metric: Train Loss and Validation Loss (log scale)
nodes.trend: Both train and val loss converge rapidly in first 10 epochs from ~0.7 to ~0.3, then plateau stably around 0.3 through epoch 68

nodes.label: Uncertainty Calibration Plot
nodes.trend: Data clustered at predicted sigma ~0.65 with wide error spread; model underestimates uncertainty compared to perfect calibration line

nodes.label: Reliability Curve
nodes.metric: Mean absolute error vs Predicted sigma (binned)
nodes.trend: All data points cluster at predicted sigma ~0.67 with mean error ~0.25, well below the perfect reliability line; indicates overconfident uncertainty estimates

nodes.label: Energy Resolution vs True Energy
nodes.metric: Mean absolute error and Fractional energy resolution
nodes.trend: Mean error relatively flat ~0.25-0.29 at low energies, rising sharply to 0.33 at highest energies; fractional resolution increases from 0.13 to 0.25 with energy

nodes.label: Physics Gradient and dE/dlogNe Distributions
nodes.metric: Gradient and dE/dlogNe distributions; Physics strength ablation (soft vs strict)
nodes.trend: Gradient distribution peaks ~0.45 well below physics target of 1.0; strict physics produces tighter distribution near 0.45 vs broad soft physics; strong physics ablation shows near-zero gradient distribution

nodes.label: Differentiability Comparison PINN vs GEANT Surrogate
nodes.trend: PINN shows stable smooth gradient ~0.5 across all logNe; GEANT surrogate shows highly variable gradients spanning orders of magnitude; PINN significantly more differentiable

nodes.label: Physics Sensitivity Map (Jacobian)
nodes.trend: logNmu most sensitive feature (0.631), followed by logNe (0.473), Age (0.200), sec_theta (0.128); muon content dominates energy sensitivity

nodes.label: Uncertainty Rises on OOD Data (ID vs OOD Predicted Sigma Distribution)
Where Physics Breaks - Physics Constraint Violation vs logNe
Information Content vs Energy - Fisher Information vs logNe
Uncertainty Decomposition vs Energy - Aleatoric vs Epistemic Uncertainty
Energy Resolution vs True Energy - Sigma(log10E) vs log10(True Energy)
Energy Resolution vs Zenith Angle - Sigma(log10E) vs Zenith Angle (deg)
Where Physics Breaks (Zoomed) - |dE/dlogNe - 1| vs logNe in Range 4.8-5.0
Physics Sensitivity Map (Jacobian) - Feature Sensitivity Heatmap
Differentiability Comparison: ML Model vs Linear Baseline (dE/dlogNe vs logNe, range 4.8-5.0)
nodes.description: Scatter plot comparing differentiability (dE/dlogNe) of an ML model versus a linear baseline across logNe range 4.8-5.0. ML model values cluster near 0 while linear baseline is constant at ~0.88, indicating the ML model has near-zero gradient in this region.
nodes.trend: ML model near-zero, linear baseline constant ~0.88

nodes.label: Reliability Curve: Mean Absolute Error vs Predicted Sigma
nodes.description: Reliability curve showing mean absolute error (y-axis, range ~5.5-5.9) vs predicted sigma (x-axis, 0.00-0.05). The curve is nearly flat and very high, while the ideal (orange dashed) is near zero, indicating poor calibration: predicted uncertainty does not correlate with actual error.
nodes.trend: Monotonically increasing mean error (~5.5 to 5.9) as sigma increases; uncalibrated

nodes.label: Uncertainty Decomposition vs Energy: Epistemic vs Aleatoric
nodes.description: Scatter plot of uncertainty (epistemic in blue, aleatoric in orange) vs log10(E). Aleatoric uncertainty increases linearly with log energy from ~20 to ~37. Epistemic uncertainty is near zero across all energies, indicating the model is confident but has high irreducible data noise.
nodes.trend: Aleatoric increases with energy; epistemic near-zero

nodes.label: Distribution of Energy (Histogram, log10 scale, peak ~5.6-5.7)
nodes.description: Histogram of event energy distribution on a log10 scale. The distribution peaks around log10(E)=5.6-5.7 and tails off on both sides, with sparse events below 5.1 and above 5.95. Indicates the training/test dataset energy coverage.
nodes.trend: Bell-shaped with peak at log10(E)~5.65

nodes.label: Uncertainty Calibration: |Error| vs Predicted Sigma (scatter)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of absolute prediction error vs predicted sigma. Most points cluster at low sigma (~0.22-0.30) with variable error (0-0.53). A few outlier points exist at sigma ~0.53. Suggests poor uncertainty calibration: predicted sigma is not proportional to actual error.
nodes.trend: Concentrated cluster at low sigma; high variance in error

nodes.label: Differentiability Comparison: PINN vs GEANT Surrogate (linear and log scale)
nodes.description: Two-panel figure comparing dE/dlogNe for PINN (orange) and GEANT surrogate (blue) on linear (left) and log (right) scales vs logNe (-4 to 3). PINN shows stable consistent gradient ~0.6; GEANT surrogate has wide scattered variance especially in log scale, up to 10^2.
nodes.trend: PINN: stable ~0.6; GEANT surrogate: high variance

nodes.label: Energy Resolution vs True Energy: Mean |Error| and Fractional Resolution
nodes.description: Two-panel figure: left shows mean |Error| vs log10(True Energy), right shows fractional resolution. Both metrics show a U-shape with best performance (lowest error ~0.127, lowest fractional resolution ~0.076) at mid-range energies (-1 to 1), and degrading at extreme energies.
nodes.metric: Mean |Error| and Fractional Resolution vs log10(True Energy)
nodes.trend: U-shaped; best at mid-energy, degrades at extremes

nodes.label: Fisher Information Content vs Energy (logNe)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of Fisher information vs logNe. Forms a U-shape with minimum ~1.50 around logNe=0 and higher values (~1.75-1.85) at extreme logNe (~-2 and ~2+). Indicates more information content at extreme electron numbers.
nodes.trend: U-shaped; minimum at logNe~0, higher at extremes

nodes.label: OOD Detection: Uncertainty Rises on Out-of-Distribution Data (ID vs OOD sigma distribution)
nodes.description: Histogram comparing predicted sigma distributions for in-distribution (ID, blue) and out-of-distribution (OOD, orange) data. The plot appears largely empty (near-zero counts), suggesting a rendering or data issue, but the title confirms the concept that uncertainty should rise on OOD data.
nodes.metric: Count vs Predicted sigma for ID vs OOD
nodes.trend: Expected: OOD sigma higher than ID sigma; plot appears near-empty

nodes.label: Where Physics Breaks: |dE/dlogNe - 1| vs logNe with Threshold
nodes.description: Scatter plot of |dE/dlogNe - 1| vs logNe showing deviation from physical scaling law (ideal = 0). Values range ~0.2-0.75 with a red dashed threshold at 0.2. Points above threshold indicate regions where model violates physics constraints, peaking at extreme logNe and central range.
nodes.trend: High violation at logNe < -2; moderate plateau ~0.3-0.4 elsewhere

nodes.label: Physics Gradients Multi-Panel
nodes.description: Four-panel figure showing distributions of dE/dlogNe gradient under different physics regimes. Panels include: raw gradient distribution, physics sanity check, strong physics gradient distribution, and physics strength ablation comparing soft vs strict physics constraints. Physics target slope=1 marked by red dashed line; gradients cluster around 0.6-0.7.
nodes.trend: Gradient distributions consistently peak below physics target of 1; strict physics shifts distribution narrower and higher compared to soft physics

nodes.label: Reliability Curve
nodes.description: Reliability curve plotting mean absolute error vs predicted sigma. Red dashed line indicates perfect reliability (slope=1). Observed data points cluster at predicted sigma ~0.75-0.80 with mean |error| ~0.12-0.15, indicating the model overestimates uncertainty (predicted sigma much larger than actual errors).
nodes.trend: Model uncertainty is systematically overestimated; actual errors are well below the perfect calibration diagonal

nodes.label: Physics Sensitivity Map (Jacobian)
nodes.description: Heatmap showing absolute Jacobian values |dE/dx| for four input features: logNe (0.808), logNmu (0.064), Age (0.326), sec_theta (0.059). logNe is the dominant feature driving energy predictions, followed by Age; logNmu and sec_theta have minimal sensitivity.
nodes.trend: logNe dominates sensitivity at 0.808; Age is secondary at 0.326; logNmu and sec_theta contribute minimally

nodes.label: Uncertainty Calibration Scatter
nodes.description: Scatter plot of absolute error vs predicted sigma for all test samples. Red dashed line shows perfect calibration. The predicted sigma is concentrated near 0.8 for all samples, while absolute errors span 0.0 to ~0.6. Indicates the model assigns similar uncertainty to all samples regardless of actual error magnitude.
nodes.trend: Uncertainty predictions lack dispersion; model collapses sigma to a narrow band around 0.8 regardless of per-sample difficulty

nodes.label: Uncertainty Decomposition vs Energy
nodes.description: Line plot decomposing uncertainty into aleatoric and epistemic components as a function of log10(E). Epistemic uncertainty dominates (~0.52-0.64) and peaks around log10(E)~0.2, then decreases at high energies. Aleatoric uncertainty remains low and stable (~0.14-0.21), slightly higher at extreme energy ends.
nodes.trend: Epistemic uncertainty peaks at intermediate energies and declines at extremes; aleatoric uncertainty is low and relatively flat with slight uptick at energy extremes

nodes.label: Distribution of logNe
nodes.description: Histogram of the logNe (log of electron number) feature in the dataset. Distribution is broadly spread from -1.6 to 2.0, with higher counts at lower logNe values (~-1.5 to -1.0, peak count ~37) and a decreasing trend toward higher values.
nodes.trend: logNe distribution is left-skewed with higher density at lower values, tapering off toward high logNe

nodes.label: Monotonic Behavior: Predicted Energy vs logNe
nodes.description: Scatter plot of predicted energy vs logNe showing the monotonic relationship enforced by physics constraints. Predicted energy increases monotonically with logNe across the range -1.5 to 2.0, with predicted energy spanning ~5.2 to 5.9.
nodes.trend: Monotonically increasing predicted energy with logNe, consistent with physics-informed constraint dE/dlogNe > 0

nodes.label: Physics Law Validation (dE/dlogNe)
nodes.description: Histogram of dE/dlogNe gradient values for physics law validation. Distribution is sharply peaked near 0.0-0.05, with a long tail extending to ~0.2. Red dashed line marks the physics target slope of 1.0. The model gradients fall far below the target, indicating partial physics compliance.
nodes.trend: Model gradients are concentrated near zero with long tail, far below physics target of 1; physics constraint not fully enforced in this validation

nodes.label: Distribution of sec(theta)
nodes.description: Histogram of the sec(theta) (secant of zenith angle) feature in the dataset. Distribution is approximately uniform from -1.6 to 1.9, with counts ranging 14-39, and a slight peak at -1.5 (~39 counts). Represents zenith angle coverage of cosmic ray shower data.
nodes.trend: Nearly uniform distribution of sec(theta) with slight excess at most negative values

nodes.label: Training History
nodes.description: Log-scale loss curve showing train and validation loss over epochs. Both train loss and validation loss drop sharply within the first 2-3 epochs from ~1.0 to below 0.01, then converge. Training appears to complete very quickly with rapid convergence.
nodes.trend: Rapid convergence within first few epochs; train and validation loss track closely, no evidence of overfitting

nodes.label: True vs Predicted Energy (Scatter Plot)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of True log10(E) vs Predicted log10(E) for an energy regression model. Range 15-17 on both axes. Red dashed ideal line shown. Points generally follow ideal but with scatter, indicating moderate prediction accuracy.
nodes.trend: positive_correlation_with_scatter

nodes.label: ML Model Comparison Table (Top 5 Boosting Models)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot of a model performance comparison table showing top 5 models: CatBoost (RMSE=0.108405, MAE=0.084101, R2=0.666007), XGBoost (RMSE=0.108608, MAE=0.084397, R2=0.664755), LightGBM (RMSE=0.109108, MAE=0.084844, R2=0.661659), Gradient Boosting (RMSE=0.109497, MAE=0.085791, R2=0.659243), Random Forest (RMSE=0.111329, MAE=0.086259, R2=0.647743). CatBoost is best performer.
nodes.trend: CatBoost_best

nodes.label: Full ML Model Comparison Table (15 Models)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot showing full regression model benchmark table with 15 models (rows 0-14): Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso, ElasticNet, Bayesian Ridge (all near R2=0.63), KNN (R2=0.598), Decision Tree (R2=0.614), Random Forest (R2=0.648), Extra Trees (R2=0.645), Gradient Boosting (R2=0.659), AdaBoost (R2=0.518), Bagging (R2=0.615), XGBoost (R2=0.665), LightGBM (R2=0.662), CatBoost (R2=0.666). CatBoost and XGBoost are top performers.
nodes.trend: ensemble_models_outperform_linear

nodes.label: PINN Reconstruction Scatter + Error Distribution (Nov 29)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot showing PINN (Physics-Informed Neural Network) Reconstruction with RMSE=0.106. Left: scatter plot of True vs Predicted log10(Energy) ranging 4.75-6.25, dense cloud near ideal line. Right: Error distribution histogram (Pred - True) showing near-normal distribution centered near 0 with slight positive skew, count up to ~5200.
nodes.trend: near_gaussian_error_distribution

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t. Muons Histogram (Nov 29)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot of histogram titled 'Gradient of Energy w.r.t Muons (should be > 0)'. X-axis: d(Energy)/d(log10_Nmu) ranging 0.0 to ~0.5+. Red dashed vertical line at 0. Histogram bars are mostly positive (right of 0), confirming physical constraint that energy increases with muon count. Peak around 0.35-0.40.
nodes.trend: positive_gradient_majority

nodes.label: Model Performance Metrics (Energy Binned) Bar Chart
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot of bar chart titled 'Model Performance Metrics (Energy Binned)'. Four bars for Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score all equal at 0.59. Uniform performance across classification metrics when energy is discretized into bins.
nodes.trend: uniform_classification_performance

nodes.label: Confusion Matrix (Energy Bins)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot of confusion matrix heatmap for energy bin classification (bins 0-8 on both axes). Color scale from 0 (purple) to ~9000 (yellow). Strong diagonal signal indicating correct bin predictions. Bin 5 has highest count (~9000). Off-diagonal spread mainly in adjacent bins, showing systematic neighboring-bin confusion.
nodes.trend: diagonal_dominant_adjacent_bin_confusion

nodes.label: PINN Reconstruction Scatter + Error Distribution (Nov 30, copy A)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot (duplicate/reshare) of PINN Reconstruction plot with RMSE=0.106. Same dual-panel figure as Nov 29 18:53:59 image: left scatter (True vs Predicted log10 Energy), right error distribution histogram centered near 0.
nodes.trend: near_gaussian_error_distribution

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t. Muons Histogram (Nov 30)
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot (duplicate/reshare) of gradient histogram titled 'Gradient of Energy w.r.t Muons (should be > 0)'. Identical to Nov 29 18:54:07 image. Red dashed line at 0, positive-skewed histogram with peak ~0.35-0.40, confirming physical constraint satisfaction.
nodes.trend: positive_gradient_majority

nodes.label: Classification Results with Timing Table
nodes.description: WhatsApp screenshot of terminal output '=== CLASSIFICATION RESULTS (WITH TIMING) ==='. Table with columns: Model, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, Train Time (s), Pred Time (s). 10 models listed: CatBoost_Clf (Acc=0.617, F1=0.600, Train=2.82s), LGBM_Clf (Acc=0.611, Train=16.07s), XGB_Clf (Acc=0.614, Train=2.73s), GradientBoosting_Clf (Acc=0.612, Train=157.09s), RandomForest_Clf (Acc=0.597, Train=54.55s), ExtraTrees_Clf (Acc=0.597, Train=17.14s), Bagging_Clf (Acc=0.591, Train=54.28s), DecisionTree_Clf (Acc=0.591, Train=1.28s), AdaBoost_Clf (Acc=0.571, Train=17.65s), KNN_Clf (Acc=0.569, Train=0.25s). CatBoost fastest+best.
nodes.metric: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, Train Time, Pred Time
nodes.trend: CatBoost_best_accuracy_and_efficient_training

nodes.label: Model Performance Metrics (Energy Binned) - Bar Chart showing Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score all at 0.59
Confusion Matrix (Energy Bins) - Heatmap with 9 bins (0-8), diagonal concentration around bin 5 with peak ~9000 counts

Final Results Table sorted by performance: CatBoost (RMSE=0.1054, R2=0.7251) top, ResNet (RMSE=0.5514, R2=-6.52) worst; includes GradientBoosting, LightGBM, XGBoost, RandomForest, ExtraTrees, FINAL_STACK_CORRECTED
ResNet PINN scatter plot: Predicted vs True Log10(E), 47.2% of events within 15% error, shows underestimation bias at low energies

SAPIT Reconstruction: RMSE=0.1071, Bias=-0.0005; scatter plot of reconstructed vs true energy log10(E/GeV) with absolute error colormap; stability across energy scale shows bias near 0 for energies >4.9 log10(E/GeV)

Model comparison table with RMSE, MAE, R2, Train Time, Pred Time: CatBoost (RMSE=0.1055, R2=0.7161, train=5.4s), XGBoost, LightGBM, GradientBoosting, RandomForest, ExtraTrees, ElasticNet, BayesianRidge, Ridge, LinearRegression, Lasso, Bagging, DecisionTree, KNN, AdaBoost

PyTorch code snippet with imports (torch, torch.nn, torch.optim, pandas, numpy, matplotlib, json, yaml, argparse, logging, pathlib, datetime, typing, sklearn) combined with partial scatter plot (Predicted vs True Log10(E)) for ResNet/PINN model

ZERO-TOLERANCE PIPELINE COMPLETED: model table (CatBoost RMSE=0.1054 R2=0.7253, LightGBM, XGBoost, GB, ET, DiffSurrogate, RF, PINN, Ensemble, Final); Bias vs Energy and Resolution vs Energy plots for XGBoost, DiffSurrogate, Ensemble

PIPELINE COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY: model table with Ensemble (RMSE=0.1053, R2=0.7254), LightGBM, CNN, MLP, CatBoost, XGBoost, SurrogateNN, RF, ET, GB, PINN; Bias vs Energy (Calibrated->0) and Resolution vs Energy plots for XGBoost, PINN, SurrogateNN
Extended model results table: Final (RMSE=0.1010, MAPE=0.0142, R2=0.7479, Bias=-0.0013), CatBoost, ResidualLearning, LightGBM, XGBoost, GB, RF; includes MAPE, Bias_Std, Time(s), Status columns
CatBoost Diagnostic Plots (Pred vs True, Residuals, QQ, Bias vs Energy, Resolution)
T50++ Ultimate Pipeline Final Evaluation (9 Models)

nodes.label: Extensive Air Shower (EAS) Development Schematic for Cosmic Ray Primary

properties.caption: Schematic of EAS development for a CR primary [97]

nodes.label: PINN Bias vs True Energy and Resolution vs True Energy

properties.bias_trend: Decreasing from ~0.9 at log10E=15 to ~-0.9 at log10E=17

nodes.label: PINN Bias Stability Across Energy Scale

properties.bias_trend: Decreasing from ~0.9 at log10E=15 to ~-1.0 at log10E=17 with error bars

nodes.label: PINN Model Performance Metrics - Energy Binned Classification

properties.note: Low classification metrics indicate energy-binned multi-class difficulty

nodes.label: Confusion Matrix (Energy Bins)
nodes.description: 10x10 confusion matrix of PINN energy bin predictions vs true energy bins. Color scale 0-3 counts. Shows bin classification performance across predicted vs true energy bins (0-9).

nodes.label: Error Distribution (Reconstruction Error)
nodes.description: Histogram of reconstruction error (Pred - True) ranging from approximately -1.75 to 2.0. Distribution is roughly symmetric and centered near 0, with peak counts of 6 around 0 and 0.9.

nodes.label: Energy Resolution (15% Fraction)
nodes.description: Line plot of fraction of predictions within 15% of true energy vs true log10(E). Energy range log10(E) 15.0 to 17.0. Peak fraction ~0.30 around log10(E)=15.75, secondary peak ~0.20 at 16.6.

nodes.label: Gradient Sanity Check
nodes.description: Histogram of approximate gradient d(pred)/d(true) ranging from -2000 to 2000. Strongly peaked near 0 (count ~35), indicating most gradients are near zero with heavy tails.

nodes.label: v8: Pred vs True (RMSE: 0.107) & Error Distribution
nodes.description: Dual panel: Left scatter plot of predicted log10(E) vs true log10(E) for PINN v8 with RMSE=0.107, red dashed ideal line. Right histogram of prediction error (Pred-True) centered near 0, range -0.4 to 0.6, peak count ~2500.

nodes.label: Uncertainty Calibration — Normalized Residuals (v8)
nodes.description: Histogram of standardized residuals (Pred-True)/sigma for PINN v8. Bell-shaped distribution centered at 0, range -4 to 5, peak count ~2000. Assesses uncertainty calibration quality.

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v8)
nodes.description: Histogram of dE/d(log10 Nmu) raw gradient for PINN v8. Distribution concentrated between 0.010-0.025, peak ~490 at ~0.021. Red dashed line at 0. Confirms positive monotonic relationship between muon number and energy.

nodes.label: Training Loss Components (v8)
nodes.description: Log-scale training loss curves over 250 epochs for PINN v8 showing 6 components: Total, Data (NLL), Var Reg, Monotonicity, Slope FD, GRAPES Calib, Heitler. Slope FD starts highest (~10^4) and converges to ~1. All components stabilize after ~50 epochs.

nodes.label: Physics Lambda Warm-up (v8)
nodes.description: Line plot of physics regularization lambda warm-up schedule over 250 epochs for PINN v8. Lambda_mono ramps to 0.030 (highest), lambda_heit stabilizes at 0.010, lambda_slope at 0.006, lambda_calib near 0, lambda_var near 0. Warm-up completes by ~epoch 20.

nodes.label: v9: Pred vs True (RMSE=0.107) & Error Distribution
nodes.description: Dual panel: Left scatter plot of predicted log10(E) vs true log10(E) for PINN v9 with RMSE=0.107, red dashed ideal line. Right histogram of prediction error (Pred-True) centered near 0, range -0.4 to 0.5, peak count ~2350. Slightly wider spread than v8.

attributes.notes: Slope FD dominates early; all losses stabilize by ~epoch 100

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v9)
v10: Pred vs True (RMSE=0.106) and Error Distribution
Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v10)

attributes.notes: Energy-bin Bias term added relative to v9; settles ~epoch 50

attributes.change_from_v9: λ_comp replaced by λ_biasE; λ_calib reduced from 0.05 to 0.02

nodes.label: v11: Pred vs True (RMSE=0.106) and Error Distribution

attributes.notes: Same RMSE as v10 but error distribution has a sharper peak and more pronounced right tail

attributes.description: Physics-Informed Neural Network reconstructing cosmic ray primary energy (log10 E) from muon number (log10 Nmu) in the GRAPES-3 experiment
Calibration of predicted uncertainty σ via standardized residuals (Pred-True)/σ across model versions
Enforcing physical monotonicity: energy must increase with muon count; gradient dE/d(log10 Nmu) must be positive
Gradual warm-up of physics constraint weights (lambdas) over first ~25 epochs to stabilize training
attributes.strategy: linear ramp from 0 to target value

nodes.label: Energy-bin Bias Loss Component (new in v10)

attributes.description: A new regularization term added in v10 to penalize systematic bias within energy bins, replacing the composition constraint from v9

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v11)

attributes.distribution_shape: sharp left-skewed spike, strongly negative gradients

attributes.observation: Slope FD starts high (~30), converges rapidly; most components stabilize by epoch 50

nodes.label: v12: Pred vs True (RMSE=0.106) and Error Distribution

attributes.distribution_shape: approximately Gaussian, centered near 0, slightly broader than v11

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v12)

attributes.distribution_shape: bell-shaped, centered around -0.020, wider spread than v11

attributes.observation: Additional CMF and Total FD components visible; convergence pattern similar to v11 but with more components

attributes.distribution_shape: approximately Gaussian, slightly right-skewed, centered near 0

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (v13)

attributes.distribution_shape: sharp spike near 0, nearly centered at zero — major shift from v11/v12 negative gradients

properties.notable_behavior: Heitler Slope and Monotonicity losses rise after convergence; Data (NLL) spikes then drops sharply; Calibration and Energy-bin Bias remain low and stable

nodes.label: PINN v13 Pred vs True & Error Distribution
PINN v14 Uncertainty Calibration — Normalized Residuals

properties.distribution_shape: bell-shaped, slightly wider than standard normal, centered near 0

properties.new_component_vs_v13: Var Reg added; Heitler Slope renamed to Heitler
properties.notable_behavior: All loss components plateau near initial values; Monotonicity rises gradually; Calibration and Energy-bin Bias remain low; Total, Data (NLL), CRPS, Var Reg nearly coincide at ~0.9

nodes.label: PINN v14 Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu

properties.distribution_shape: right-skewed unimodal, mostly positive gradients indicating positive correlation between Nmu and Energy
properties.physics_interpretation: Monotonicity constraint: energy increases with muon number (log10 Nmu)

nodes.label: PINN iter-16 Shape 0: SDF Loss & Eikonal Loss

properties.observation: Baseline SDF converges lower than +Eikonal variant for Shape 0; Eikonal constraint reduces SDF fitting accuracy

nodes.label: PINN iter-16 Shape 1: SDF Loss & Eikonal Loss

properties.observation: Baseline and +Eikonal SDF losses converge to similar final values for Shape 1

nodes.label: PINN iter-16 Shape 2: SDF Loss & Eikonal Loss

properties.observation: Similar convergence for both variants; both reach lower SDF loss than Shape 0 or Shape 1

nodes.label: PINN iter-16 Shape 3: SDF Loss & Eikonal Loss

properties.observation: Eikonal constraint causes Shape 3 +Eikonal SDF loss to plateau 25x higher than baseline; Baseline dominates significantly

nodes.label: Ultra-PINN v14 Pred vs True & Error Distribution

properties.note: Same RMSE as v13 (0.106) but slightly larger bias (-0.003 vs -0.001)

properties.concept: Iterative refinement of Physics-Informed Neural Network from v13 to v14
properties.key_change: Addition of Var Reg loss and reformulation of Heitler Slope to Heitler in v14

nodes.label: Eikonal Loss for Signed Distance Function

properties.concept: Eikonal equation constraint enforces |grad(SDF)|=1 as physics prior in SDF-PINN
properties.general_finding: Eikonal constraint can help regularize SDF geometry but may hurt final SDF accuracy depending on shape complexity

properties.task: Predict log10(E) of cosmic ray primary from shower observables

nodes.label: NLL and CRPS Probabilistic Loss Functions

properties.concept: Negative Log-Likelihood and Continuous Ranked Probability Score used for probabilistic output training
properties.role: Data fidelity terms encouraging well-calibrated uncertainty estimates

properties.concept: Heitler model relates cosmic ray energy to shower particle multiplicity via power-law scaling
properties.role: Physics-informed regularization in PINN loss (Heitler Slope in v13, Heitler in v14)

properties.concept: Physics constraint enforcing that predicted energy monotonically increases with muon number log10(Nmu)
properties.evidence: dE/d(log10 Nmu) distribution is predominantly positive (peak ~0.25-0.30)

nodes.label: Training Loss Components (Iter 5)
nodes.description: Log-scale training loss curves over ~270 epochs for PINN iteration 5. Components: Total, Data (NLL), Var Reg, Monotonicity, Slope FD, GRAPES Calib, Heitler. Monotonicity loss drops sharply from ~1e0 to ~1e-8 by epoch 150.

nodes.label: Automatic Lambda Balancing (Iter 5)
nodes.description: Automatic lambda balancing over ~270 epochs for iteration 5. lambda_mono peaks ~0.37 at epoch ~3 then decays to ~0.17. lambda_calib stabilises ~0.033. lambda_var, lambda_slope, lambda_heitler remain near zero.

nodes.label: PINN+GRAPES+Heitler+Unc v2 Prediction Scatter (Iter 5, RMSE 0.106)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of predicted vs true log10(E) for PINN+GRAPES+Heitler+Unc v2, iteration 5. RMSE=0.106. Accompanying error distribution (Pred-True) is approximately Gaussian, centred near 0, range roughly -0.4 to +0.6.

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (Iter 5)
nodes.description: Histogram of dE/d(log10 Nmu) [raw] for iteration 5. Distribution is bell-shaped, centred ~0.75-0.80, range 0.0 to 1.4. All gradients positive — consistent with monotonic energy-Nmu relationship. Count peaks ~6000.

nodes.label: PINN+GRAPES+Heitler+Unc v4 Prediction Scatter (Iter 6, RMSE 0.202)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of predicted vs true log10(E) for PINN+GRAPES+Heitler+Unc v4, iteration 6. RMSE=0.202 — significantly worse than iteration 5. Predictions form horizontal banding artefacts suggesting quantisation or discretisation issues. Error distribution is heavily left-skewed (range -3.5 to +0.5).

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy w.r.t log10 Nmu (Iter 6)
nodes.description: Histogram of dE/d(log10 Nmu) [raw] for iteration 6. All counts spike sharply at ~1.1, with negligible spread — gradient has collapsed to a near-constant value. This is pathological and correlates with the banding artefact and high RMSE in iter 6.

nodes.label: Training Loss Components (Iter 6)
nodes.description: Log-scale training loss curves over ~300 epochs for PINN iteration 6. Slope FD loss is highly volatile throughout (range 1e-1 to 1e4). Heitler loss drops sharply then stabilises near 1e-6. Data (NLL) and Var Reg stabilise near 1. GRAPES Calib near 3e-1.

nodes.label: Physics Lambda Warm-up std-normalised (Iter 6)
nodes.description: Physics lambda warm-up (std-normalised) over ~300 epochs for iteration 6. lambda_mono ramps up steeply and plateaus at ~0.148 by epoch 10. lambda_heitler plateaus ~0.049. lambda_slope ~0.029. lambda_var and lambda_calib near zero. Fixed schedule vs automatic balancing in iter 5.

nodes.label: Gradient of Energy wrt log10_Nmu (Iter 7)
nodes.description: Histogram of dE/d(log10_Nmu) for iteration 7. Distribution is broader and centred near 0, with a right tail up to ~4. Some negative gradients present (left of dashed red line at 0) — partial monotonicity violations remain. Peak count ~130 near 0.3.

nodes.label: PINN+GRAPES+Heitler Prediction Scatter (Iter 7, RMSE 0.110)
nodes.description: Scatter plot of predicted vs true Log10(E) for PINN+GRAPES+Heitler (no Unc), iteration 7. RMSE=0.110 — slightly worse than iter 5 (0.106), better than iter 6 (0.202). Error distribution approximately Gaussian, slightly left-shifted, range -0.4 to +0.8.

nodes.label: PINN + GRAPES Framework
nodes.description: Physics-Informed Neural Network combined with GRAPES cosmic-ray shower simulation calibration. Core framework across all three iterations.

nodes.label: Uncertainty Estimation (Unc)
nodes.description: Uncertainty output head present in iterations 5 (v2) and 6 (v4) but absent in iteration 7. Reflected in model variant naming convention.

nodes.label: Cosmic-Ray Energy Reconstruction
nodes.description: Primary task: regressing log10(E) from GRAPES shower observables. Performance measured by RMSE. Best result across these iterations: 0.106 (iter 5).

nodes.label: Ferriere Deep Ensemble GNN for GRAND
Varsi GEANT4 GRAPES-3 Muon Telescope JINST

links.note: Both show differentiability comparison (dE/dlogNe vs logNe) for ML models; download_010931 is a narrower logNe range version of fig_differentiability
Both measure dE/dlogNe as a diagnostic of physical consistency of the model
Both assess uncertainty calibration via predicted sigma vs actual error, using different visualization styles (reliability curve vs scatter)
Both use predicted sigma as the key metric for evaluating model uncertainty behavior
Uncertainty decomposition (epistemic/aleatoric) directly informs OOD detection capability; high epistemic would signal OOD
Both analyze information content vs energy: Fisher info measures theoretical bound, uncertainty decomposition measures practical model uncertainty
Energy distribution shapes the x-axis coverage in uncertainty vs energy decomposition plot
Both characterize model prediction quality vs energy; resolution links to aleatoric uncertainty magnitude
Energy distribution defines the data density across energy bins; energy resolution performance correlates with data density
Both examine predicted sigma distributions and uncertainty quality; OOD detection extends calibration to out-of-distribution data
Physics breaks figure is derived from differentiability: it plots |dE/dlogNe - 1|, directly computed from the differentiability comparison data
Fisher information provides the theoretical lower bound on resolution; energy resolution plot shows empirical achieved resolution
Both use logNe as x-axis and evaluate model fidelity at different electron number scales; Fisher info and physics consistency both peak at extremes

links.description: Both files compare model metrics; CatBoost appears in both with identical RMSE/MAE/R2 values
Same set of models (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, RF, GB, PINN, Ensemble) appear in both; T50++ is an extended pipeline version
PINN model listed in comparison table; CosmicNet-PINN is the detailed PINN evaluation
HPO optimization feeds into final CatBoost metrics shown in the comparison table
Classification metrics complement regression metrics (RMSE, MAE, R2) shown in comparison table for the PINN model
CatBoost is one of the 9 models in the T50++ pipeline; diagnostic plots detail CatBoost performance
HPO results define CatBoost hyperparameters; diagnostic plots show CatBoost model performance after tuning
Both show bias vs. energy and resolution vs. energy diagnostic plots, one for PINN and one for CatBoost
PINN (SuperDiffSurrogate) is model #7 in T50++ pipeline; CosmicNet-PINN evaluates its performance
The T50++ pipeline models reconstruct cosmic ray energy from EAS observables
National compute capacity informs feasibility context for large-scale ML pipelines like T50++ in different countries
HPO-tuned CatBoost hyperparameters feed into the T50++ pipeline as one of its 9 constituent models
EAS development is the physical process that CosmicNet-PINN is trained to reconstruct energy from
Bias and resolution plots are sub-diagnostics of the PINN model evaluation shown in CosmicNet-PINN comprehensive evaluation
Stability plot is a diagnostic for the CosmicNet-PINN model
Classification metrics (accuracy, precision, recall) are energy-binned performance metrics for the PINN model
EAS physics defines the energy scale (log10(E)) used as axis in PINN bias and resolution plots
Both show bias vs. true energy for the PINN model; stability plot adds error bars to the same trend

links.rationale: Both are PINN model evaluation diagnostics measuring prediction accuracy on energy bins/reconstruction
Both measure energy bin classification/resolution quality of the PINN model
Error distribution and 15% fraction metric both quantify the spread/accuracy of energy reconstruction
Both plot reconstruction/prediction error histograms; the standalone error_distribution appears to be an earlier/simpler version of v8's error distribution panel
15% energy resolution fraction is an alternative metric to RMSE for assessing the same PINN energy prediction quality
Both are gradient sanity/physics checks — the earlier gradient_check plots d(pred)/d(true) while v8 plots dE/d(log10 Nmu), both verifying physics constraints
Gradient sanity check validates that physics-informed loss terms (monotonicity, slope FD) produce well-behaved gradients during training

v9 evaluation results reflect the training procedure used in v8, as both share the same architecture and loss components with minor updates
